semgrep_findings.message: Detected a dynamic value being used with urllib. urllib supports 'file://' schemes, so a dynamic value controlled by a malicious actor may allow them to read arbitrary files. Audit uses of urllib calls to ensure user data cannot control the URLs, or consider using the 'requests' library instead.
semgrep_findings.remediation: Review and apply the rule guidance: https://semgrep.dev/r/python.lang.security.audit.dynamic-urllib-use-detected.dynamic-urllib-use-detected

semgrep_findings.message: By not specifying a USER, a program in the container may run as 'root'. This is a security hazard. If an attacker can control a process running as root, they may have control over the container. Ensure that the last USER in a Dockerfile is a USER other than 'root'.
semgrep_findings.remediation: Review and apply the rule guidance: https://semgrep.dev/r/dockerfile.security.missing-user-entrypoint.missing-user-entrypoint

semgrep_findings.remediation: Review and apply the rule guidance: https://semgrep.dev/r/dockerfile.security.missing-user.missing-user

semgrep_findings.message: Running flask app with host 0.0.0.0 could expose the server publicly.
semgrep_findings.remediation: Review and apply the rule guidance: https://semgrep.dev/r/python.flask.security.audit.app-run-param-config.avoid_app_run_with_bad_host

waivers.what: JWT header placeholder (eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...) in the old .env.example, commit 002bb0f
waivers.why: Documentation placeholder — the standard JWT header prefix, not a real credential. Frozen in git history.

waivers.what: node_modules/, dist/ and package-lock.json excluded from secret scanning
waivers.why: Generated/vendored files — integrity hashes trigger entropy-based rules with no real secrets.
